title: silene vulgaris subsp. maritima
intro: Silene vulgaris subsp. maritima, also known as coastal catchfly, is a subspecies of the Silene vulgaris plant, belonging to the carnation family (Caryophyllaceae). This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of coastal catchfly include its ability to reduce inflammation, alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues, and even help manage diabetes. In herbal preparations, coastal catchfly is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures to harness its medicinal properties, although further research is necessary to fully understand its potential benefits and potential interactions with other medications.
